industry.headline: Cypress Creek Secures $3.5 Billion for Steel River Energy Center
industry.summary: Cypress Creek Energy, a U.S.-based solar and energy storage project developer, has secured $3.5 billion in financing. This funding is designated for the first two phases of the Steel River Energy Center project. The developer focuses on solar and energy storage solutions to support the project's development and financial requirements for this specific energy center initiative.

industry.headline: Military Engineer Services Issues O&M Tender for 6 MW Solar
industry.summary: Military Engineer Services (MES) has invited bids for the comprehensive operation and maintenance (O&M) of solar projects. The tender covers a 4 MW and a 2 MW solar project located at the Army area in Jodhpur, Rajasthan. These combined 6 MW of projects require professional O&M services to ensure continued operational efficiency within the military installation.

industry.headline: Bihar Regulator Approves Tariffs for 275 MW Rooftop Solar
industry.summary: Bihar Electricity Regulatory Commission (BERC) has approved tariffs discovered through competitive bidding for rooftop solar power. The procurement involves nearly 275 MW of rooftop solar power from approximately 250,000 Kutir Jyoti consumers across Bihar. This regulatory approval facilitates the procurement of solar energy from a large number of residential consumers under the specific Kutir Jyoti scheme.

tech.headline: Sierra Club Urges Climate Accountability in NYC Pension Asset Search
tech.summary: Sierra Club welcomes the search for asset managers for New York City’s five public pension systems announced by Comptroller Mark Levine and trustees. The organization urges that passive indexing service mandates be awarded only to firms demonstrating responsible stewardship and credible climate-risk management. The search opens a review of major asset managers to ensure strong climate accountability within the city's pension systems.

tech.headline: New cooling panel uses PV and waste cooking oil
tech.summary: Researchers have designed an innovative home cooling panel system. The design incorporates PV power generation, thermoelectric cooling, and latent heat storage using waste cooking oil. Through optimized modeling, results suggest that a single panel can deliver approximately 6–15 kWh/day of cooling, providing a potential new method for residential temperature management using renewable energy.

tech.headline: Chile sets bidding terms for first energy auction including storage
tech.summary: Chile’s CNE has released preliminary terms for the 2026/01 power supply tender. This technology-neutral process favors renewables by excluding high-emission fuels and allowing storage-backed bids. Utilizing 15-year contracts and time-of-day demand blocks, the structure is designed to support photovoltaic and hybrid solar-plus-storage projects, specifically targeting daytime and evening peak coverage requirements.
tech.searchQuery: Chile releases bidding terms for first energy auct 2026

tech.headline: SNEC 2026 shows solar shifting toward system integration and AI
tech.summary: SNEC 2026 highlighted a significant industry shift from module-centric growth toward system integration. Storage and AI are becoming increasingly central to project design and value creation. At the technology level, back-contact technology gained momentum as a premium platform, while emerging applications and scenario-specific modules signaled a trend toward deeper market segmentation within the solar sector.
tech.searchQuery: SNEC 2026 wrap-up: Storage, AI and back-contact ar 2026

tech.headline: Longi develops 27.27% efficient heterojunction back-contact solar cell
tech.summary: Longi and Yangzhou University researchers developed a laser-based manufacturing strategy for HJT back-contact solar cells. This method mitigates shock-wave-induced damage, improving passivation stability and device performance. The approach resulted in a certified 27.27% efficient solar cell, providing an industrially viable pathway toward the fabrication of ultra-high-efficiency photovoltaic technology through improved laser processing techniques.

tech.headline: Perforated Hexagonal‑Fin Heat Sinks Boost PV Module Cooling
tech.summary: Algerian researchers simulated perforated hexagonal‑fin heat sinks for photovoltaic modules using CFD analysis, identifying a design that lowered cell temperature by up to 20.93% and increased module efficiency while maintaining strong thermal performance under forced convection conditions.

learn.title: India's Green Hydrogen Mission: Clean Energy Targets
learn.intro: India's National Green Hydrogen Mission aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030, reducing reliance on fossil fuels and cutting carbon emissions. This initiative positions India as a global leader in clean energy innovation.
learn.c1_text: Green hydrogen is produced via electrolysis using renewable energy, emitting zero carbon. For example, 1 kg of green hydrogen prevents ~9 kg of CO2 emissions compared to gray hydrogen.
learn.c2_text: The mission offers subsidies covering 50% of capital costs for electrolyzers and renewable energy projects, encouraging private investment. This reduces production costs by up to 20%.
learn.c3_text: India plans to install 125 GW of renewable energy by 2030 to power electrolyzers. The mission targets 4 million tonnes of green hydrogen by 2030, with pilot projects in Tamil Nadu and Gujarat.
learn.stat1: India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030
learn.stat2: Subsidies cover 50% of electrolyzer capital costs
learn.stat4: 4 million tonnes green hydrogen production target by 2030

learn.title: Solar Manufacturing Incentives Boost Local Production
learn.intro: India's Production Linked Incentive (PLI) scheme boosts domestic solar manufacturing, reducing import dependence and creating jobs. This policy supports India's goal of 500 GW renewable energy by 2030.
learn.c1_text: The PLI scheme offers 4-6% incentives on annual sales for solar module and cell manufacturers. This has attracted companies like Adani and Tata to invest ₹10,000 crores in new plants.
learn.c2_text: Since 2020, India's solar module production has surged from 2 GW to 40 GW annually. The PLI scheme aims to reach 100 GW domestic manufacturing capacity by 2025.
learn.c3_text: India's solar capacity reached 67 GW by 2024. The PLI scheme supports the national target of 280 GW renewable energy by 2030, with 90% of solar modules now produced domestically.
learn.stat1: 40 GW annual solar module production in India (2024)
learn.stat2: ₹10,000 crores invested in PLI-linked solar plants

learn.title: EVs as Grid Stabilizers via Vehicle-to-Grid Tech
learn.intro: Vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology allows electric vehicles to feed power back to the grid, balancing supply and demand. This innovation enhances grid resilience and supports renewable energy integration.
learn.c1_text: V2G systems use bidirectional chargers to convert DC from EV batteries to AC for the grid. A typical EV battery (60 kWh) can power a home for 2 days during outages.
learn.c2_text: V2G aggregates multiple EVs into virtual power plants. During peak demand, 100 EVs can provide 6 MW of power, equivalent to a small gas power plant.
learn.c3_text: India has 10 million EVs on roads by 2025. With 40% battery capacity utilization, this could provide 24 GW of flexible power, supporting 80% renewable energy integration.
learn.stat1: 10 million EVs projected in India by 2025
learn.stat2: 6 MW power from 100 EVs in V2G
learn.stat3: 24 GW flexible power potential from Indian EV fleet

learn.title: How Wind Turbines Generate Electricity
learn.intro: Wind turbines convert kinetic energy from wind into electricity using aerodynamic blades and generators. This clean energy source powers millions of homes globally.
learn.c1_text: Blades shaped like airfoils create lift, spinning the rotor at 10-15 rpm. A 2 MW turbine's blades sweep an area larger than a football field (10,000 m²).
learn.c2_text: The rotor's mechanical energy turns a generator via a gearbox (15-20x speed increase). Modern turbines use permanent magnet generators for 95% efficiency.
learn.c3_text: India has 43 GW wind capacity (2024), with Tamil Nadu leading at 10 GW. The government targets 140 GW renewable energy by 2030, including 75 GW wind.
learn.stat2: 10,000 m² swept area per 2 MW turbine
learn.stat4: 75 GW wind target in India's 2030 plan
learn.readLabel: Read at US Dept of Energy

learn.title: Floating Solar Farms: Water Conservation Benefits
learn.intro: Floating solar farms on reservoirs and lakes reduce water evaporation by 70%, conserve land, and boost efficiency. This technology is transforming water-scarce regions.
learn.c1_text: Floating solar reduces water evaporation by 70-80%. A 1 GW plant saves 2.5 billion liters annually, equivalent to 10,000 Olympic pools.
learn.c2_text: Cooling from water increases panel efficiency by 10-15%. Panels at 25°C operate at 22% efficiency vs. 18% at 45°C on land.
learn.c3_text: India has 5,000+ reservoirs suitable for floating solar. The first 600 MW plant in Gujarat's Kaydara Lake powers 100,000 homes while saving 1.5 billion liters/year.
learn.stat2: 2.5 billion liters saved annually per 1 GW plant
learn.stat4: 600 MW Kaydara solar plant in Gujarat

learn.title: Floating Offshore Wind for Deep Water
learn.intro: Floating offshore wind platforms use mooring systems to harness deep-water wind resources, unlocking 80% of global wind potential. This technology is critical for coastal regions.
learn.c1_text: Spar buoys, semi-submersibles, and tension-leg platforms anchor turbines 100+ meters deep. Norway's Hywind project uses spar buoys to operate in 200-meter depths.
learn.c2_text: Wind speeds increase with depth, boosting capacity factors by 20-30%. A 12 MW floating turbine generates 50 GWh/year, enough for 15,000 homes.
learn.c3_text: India aims for 50 GW offshore wind by 2030, including floating platforms in the Arabian Sea. The first 1 GW project in Gujarat could power 700,000 homes.
learn.stat1: 80% of global wind potential in deep water
learn.stat2: 20-30% higher capacity factors in deep water
learn.readLabel: Read at US Dept of Energy
